title: cassia brachiata
intro: Cassia brachiata, also known as Agaru or Cassia tree, is a small deciduous tree or shrub native to tropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cassia brachiata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Cassia brachiata is often used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including skin infections, wounds, and respiratory problems.
